name: Vegetable Samosa
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Potatoes, cut into even-size pieces
1 Piece ginger root, peeled and grated (1/2")
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil potatoes in salted water for 15-20 minutes or until tender. Drain well, return to the saucepan, and shake over low heat a few moments or until dry. Mash well.
2. Cook peas in boiling salted water for 4 minutes. Drain well.
3. Heat oil in a skillet. Add onion, cumin seeds, ginger, turmeric, Garam Masala, and salt. Cook gently for 5 minutes.
4. Add mashed potatoes and peas, then stir in lemon juice. Mix well, remove from heat, and cool.
5. Sift flour into a bowl. Cut in butter finely until the mixture resembles bread crumbs. Add milk and mix to form a stiff dough. Divide into 6 equal pieces.
6. Form each piece into a ball and roll each ball on a lightly floured surface to a 6-inch circle. Cut each circle in half.
7. Divide filling equally among semicircles of pastry.
8. Dampen edges of pastry, then fold over and seal to form triangles which enclose the filling completely.
9. Heat oil in a deep-fat fryer or saucepan to 375°F (190°C) or until a 1/2-inch cube of day-old bread browns in 40 seconds. Fry samosas in hot oil, a few at a time, for 3-4 minutes or until golden brown.
10. Drain on paper towels. Garnish with lime twists and celery leaves, if desired, and serve hot with Mango Chutney.
Makes 12 samosas.
